title: Li Auto International Expansion Strategy
text: Li Auto has established a new first-tier division dedicated to overseas market expansion, signaling a renewed commitment to international growth. The division is headed by Wang Jin, who reports to the company's senior vice president of sales and service. The company is focusing on the Middle East and Latin America. Wang's previous experience in Chile at Huawei positions him well for Latin American operations, while the Middle East remains a priority market. Initially planning direct-store operations, Li Auto has pivoted to recruiting local dealers instead. The company cited concerns that opening company-owned stores could alienate existing channel partners and drive them toward competitors. Li Auto is developing specialized designs for overseas markets, with teams identifying parts requiring adaptation for Middle Eastern conditions. This strategic reversal follows domestic challenges, particularly the underperformance of the Li Mega battery-electric vehicle and increased domestic competition. The company had previously delayed international expansion, with leadership suggesting no serious overseas entry before 2028. Li Auto remains one of China's few major EV makers without active international operations, while competitors like Nio and Xpeng are already expanding globally.
